title: The Renown SDK provides DID-based authentication using did:pkh format with React provider, session persistence, and profile enrichment
content: The `@renown/sdk` provides decentralized identity authentication for Powerhouse apps. Core components: `RenownUserProvider` wraps the app and auto-initializes the SDK, creates `window.renown` and `window.connectCrypto` globals, checks sessionStorage for existing sessions, and handles Renown portal redirects. The `useUser()` hook returns `{ user, loginStatus, isLoading, login, logout, openRenown, connectCrypto, renown }`. Users are identified by DIDs in `did:pkh:eip155:<chainId>:<ethAddress>` format. The `User` type includes `did`, `address`, `name`, `email`, `avatar`, and `ethAddress`. Session data is stored in `sessionStorage` (not localStorage) for security -- clears on tab close. The `RenownAuthButton` is a headless component with render props for customization. LoginStatus flows: `initial` -> `checking` -> `authorized` | `not-authorized`.
description: RenownUserProvider auto-initializes SDK, manages sessions via sessionStorage, handles redirects, and enriches users with profile data.
